title: crotalaria quercetorum
intro: Crotalaria quercetorum, also known as the oak leaf rattlebox, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Crotalaria quercetorum include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an expectorant, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
